title: How Saved Filters Keep AI and Charts Consistent
summary: Charts and Talk to Data disagree when they use different row subsets. Saved filters align both.

content.summary: Charts and Talk to Data disagree when they use different row subsets. Saved filters align both.

body.text: One source of truth for row scope beats re-explaining exclusions in every prompt. Define Active status, fiscal date range, and excluded test accounts once in a saved rule. Apply the rule before charting and before asking questions so every surface analyzes the same population.

body.alt: Team collaborating around data on a screen
body.caption: Team collaborating around data on a screen. Photo: Unsplash

body.text: Define Active status, fiscal date range, and excluded test accounts once in a saved rule.
Apply the rule before charting and before asking questions so every surface analyzes the same population.

items: Name rules after the business decision they support.
Document excluded values in rule names or wiki links.

body.text: Weekly reporting becomes apply-rule, refresh charts, re-ask standing AI questions—minutes of consistency.
New analysts inherit correct scope without memorizing tribal filter knowledge.
Datilio saved rules synchronize charts, exports, and Talk to Data on one subset.

meta_description: Charts and Talk to Data disagree when they use different row subsets. Saved filters align both.
